author: mark
text:
bl init --bare: first-class bare central-hub bootstrap

Mechanizes the README 'Bootstrapping a bare hub from scratch' steps 2-3 as one idempotent command: bare-clone the source into <hub>/.git, wire the origin fetch refspec, scaffold the loose .balls store, materialize config.json from main's tree (no checkout exists to copy it from at a bare root), and attach the state worktree.

Non-destructive on the same terms as the working-tree bl init: reuses an existing bare gitdir, refuses to clobber a non-bare .git, re-creates only what is missing, never resets or force-pushes a shared branch. The working-tree-only wiring (balls: initialize commit, main .gitignore, plugins .gitkeep) is correctly skipped, not faked.

State wiring reuses setup_state_branch; cmd_init moves out of basic.rs into its own module to stay under the 300-line cap and give the bare path a clear home. README + SPEC-orphan-branch-state s10 updated, s14 conformance test 16 added; the by-hand sequence stays canonical per the standard-tools-suffice principle.
